description: Fix CDP Page.enable leak when browser commands reuse network capture's debugger session

userStories.title: Disable CDP Page domain after use in withDebugger callbacks
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want CDP commands that call Page.enable inside withDebugger to also call Page.disable before returning, so that the Page domain does not remain enabled on a shared network capture debugger session and produce unnecessary IPC traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBrowserListResources calls Page.disable before its withDebugger callback returns
handleBrowserGetResourceContent calls Page.disable before its withDebugger callback returns
handleBrowserHandleDialog calls Page.disable before its withDebugger callback returns (in a finally block so it runs even when the CDP command throws)
Page.disable is called only on the alreadyAttached path (when reusing an existing session) â€” the !alreadyAttached path detaches entirely so Page.disable is unnecessary
Existing error handling and sendSuccessResult/sendErrorResult calls are preserved unchanged
